content:
The idea that lifting your arms above your head during pregnancy will harm the baby is actually a myth. There is no scientific evidence to support the claim that raising your arms above your head can cause harm to the baby. 

During pregnancy, your body goes through many changes, and certain movements or positions may sometimes feel uncomfortable, but generally, lifting your arms above your head is safe. It can even help stretch and relieve tension in your shoulders and upper back.

However, if you experience dizziness, shortness of breath, or any discomfort when raising your arms or changing positions, it’s a good idea to sit down and rest, and consult your healthcare provider to rule out any underlying issues such as low blood pressure or circulatory concerns.

In summary:
- Raising your arms above your head does not harm the baby.
- It is usually safe and can help with muscle tension.
- If you feel unwell when doing so, seek medical advice.

If you have any specific concerns about movements or exercises during pregnancy, it’s always best to talk with your healthcare provider for personalized guidance.
